title: LOVE Package Manifest v1
description: Registry-neutral, content-addressed metadata for a publicly readable software package artifact. LOVE means Locator-independent, Open, Verifiable, Exchangeable.
$comment: Conformance validators MUST enable JSON Schema format assertion. The regular-expression constraints are defense in depth, not a replacement for URI parsing.

protocol.description: MUST equal 'love-package/v1' for this protocol version.

document_type.description: Discriminates this document from a love-package/v1 package index.

name.description: Human- and tool-facing package label. It is not content identity or proof of ownership.

license.description: Declared reuse terms. Null means no license is declared and grants no permission by implication.

absoluteHttpUrl.description: Absolute HTTP or HTTPS URL without userinfo, whitespace, or a fragment. Public Internet package hosts SHOULD use HTTPS. Resolution and every redirect remain subject to the caller's SSRF/egress policy.

format.description: Archive and layout profile. This names the npm tarball format; it does not require the npm registry.

filename.description: ASCII npm-tarball basename ending in .tgz. It contains only alphanumerics, dot, underscore, plus, and hyphen; it is not content identity.

sha256.description: Lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the exact raw artifact bytes, without a prefix.

size.description: Exact artifact byte length as a non-zero I-JSON-safe integer.

media_type.description: Media type of the exact gzip-compressed npm tarball bytes.

mirrors.description: Interchangeable public read locations expected to return the exact bytes identified by sha256 and size.

engines.description: Supported JavaScript runtime names mapped to their version constraints. Each named engine is a supported alternative. An empty object explicitly means the producer declares no engine compatibility floor.

specifier.description: Primary artifact mirror URL for URL-capable local installers. This is data, never a shell command.

revision.description: Immutable repository-native revision of the tracked source worktree used for the package release.

path.description: Normalized POSIX repository-relative path with no empty, dot, dot-dot, backslash, control-character, or absolute segment; use '.' for the repository root.

mode.description: Dependency declarations, when present, are read from the package manifest inside the verified artifact.

self_contained.description: Whether the artifact declares that it needs no separately resolved package dependencies. This does not include the named runtime engine or operating-system facilities.
